learning_objective: After solving this problem, the student will be able to identify when a longest-subarray-with-bounded-sum problem admits a two-pointer sliding window approach and implement it to achieve O(N) time complexity.
prerequisites: prefix sums and subarray sum computation
understanding of O(N) vs O(N²) complexity
story_direction: A town is preparing for a festival and must illuminate a parade route with N lanterns. Each lantern has a known power consumption. The town has a single generator with a fixed power capacity S. The goal is to find the maximum number of consecutive lanterns that can be simultaneously powered without exceeding the generator's capacity.
